narrative: Fire and disturbance history featured heavily this week: pan-boreal forest age structure was mapped as a function of wildfire and logging regimes, while a 70-year South African grassland burning experiment showed compositional resilience to a two-year drought despite fire regime driving diversity. Climate effects on productivity were quantified through strategic dam management offsetting warming-driven declines in Yellowstone cutthroat trout, and through three decades of tracking showing threatened Chinook salmon advanced rather than truncated their coastal nursery rearing phenology in warm years. Trophic interactions responded to climate too, with 24 years of data showing owl diets shift with drought-driven prey scarcity in a semi-arid Chilean ecosystem, while elevated UV-B altered anti-herbivore plant defence allocation unless offset by silicon supplementation. Behavioural work showed free-ranging lizards actively regulate ultraviolet exposure as a parallel axis to thermoregulation, and that Australia's two largest marsupial carnivores act as secondary long-distance dispersers of mycorrhizal fungal spores through their diet. Invasion ecology findings included reduced litter allelochemicals facilitating further alien establishment, supporting the invasional meltdown hypothesis, and strong phenotypic plasticity rather than local adaptation in invasive knotweed across a European common-garden network. Grazing and landscape management shaped resilience outcomes: grassland biomass stability under climate variability depended on mowing versus sheep grazing, and regenerating forestry clearcuts functioned as ecological traps for woodland caribou by combining elevated forage with elevated predation risk. In conservation practice, three decades of IUCN Red List assessments charted an extinction-risk trajectory for sharks, rays and chimaeras, while a review of conservation study designs found a gradual multi-decade shift from simple before-after comparisons toward more robust before-after-control-impact and randomised designs. Finally, range-expanding earthworms were shown to extend late-season carbon uptake in Arctic tundra, and a genomic phylogeny of 156 nematode species revealed a surprisingly late and convergent evolutionary origin of parasitism.

articles.title: Pan‐boreal mapping of forest age and implications for conservation
articles.digest: Random forest modelling combining multisource forest data mapped boreal forest age across the biome, revealing a bimodal age structure shaped by fire and logging regimes.

articles.title: Does burning history affect drought response in a South African mesic grassland?
articles.digest: A 70-year South African burning experiment found grassland plant composition stayed largely resilient to a two-year drought, though burning frequency shaped diversity and forb dominance.

articles.title: Pinus yunnanensis var. pygmaea individuals are more ready to embrace wildfire in more fire‐prone areas
articles.digest: Pinus yunnanensis var. pygmaea individuals from more fire-prone regions showed greater serotiny and other trait adaptations for wildfire resilience.

Strategic dam management can offset climate‐driven declines in fish production
articles.digest: Six decades of Bayesian modelling showed matched timing of natural and managed peak river flows reduced Yellowstone cutthroat trout productivity, while mismatched peaks increased it.

articles.title: Intact coastal nursery rearing amid climate‐driven phenological shifts in threatened salmon
articles.digest: Three decades of data on threatened Chinook salmon showed warm years advanced, rather than truncated, coastal nursery rearing phenology and growth.

articles.title: CO2 Fluxes Recover Faster from Summer Drought in Sub-arctic Sphagnum Peatland than in Tundra Heath, While Being Similar after Winter Freezing
articles.digest: Sub-arctic Sphagnum peatland recovered CO2 fluxes faster from summer drought than tundra heath, though winter freezing legacy effects were similar between ecosystem types.

articles.title: Including Fitness and Health Proxies Can Alter Our Understanding of Habitat Selection
articles.digest: A literature review and simulation showed habitat selection studies rarely include fitness or health proxies, risking misidentification of sink habitats as high quality.

articles.title: Integrating and assessing metacommunity drivers of ecosystem function and resilience in benthic algal systems
articles.digest: A framework integrated metacommunity drivers of ecosystem function and resilience, applied to benthic algal systems where dispersal and disturbance jointly regulate outcomes.

Beyond thermoregulation: Active behavioural regulation of ultraviolet light exposure by a free‐ranging lizard

articles.title: Relationship between home range and population density in mammals: the role of sociality, territoriality and habitat dimensionality
articles.digest: Mammal home range and population density scaling was shown to depend on sociality, territoriality and habitat dimensionality, not just body mass and energy needs.

articles.title: Boldness and niche width structure spatial interactions in a natural rodent community
articles.digest: Individual boldness and niche width structured spatial encounter patterns in a natural rodent community, linking personality to community-level competition.

articles.title: Spatial and Seasonal Variation in Avian Dietary Strategies
articles.digest: The first global assessment of seasonal variation in avian dietary strategies found spatial and seasonal dietary shifts remain poorly understood despite shaping biodiversity patterns.

articles.title: Unveiling the effects of interspecific competition: ecological consequences of competitive release after damming on Salvelinus curilus populations in a three-salmonid species coexistence system
articles.digest: Damming rivers altered interspecific competition among three salmonid species, with competitive release upstream boosting growth, density and foraging-related morphology in Dolly Varden.

articles.title: Contrasting cryptofaunal responses to seabird nutrient inputs illuminate coral reef productivity pathways
articles.digest: Cryptobenthic fishes and invertebrates mediated how seabird nutrient subsidies propagate through coral reef food webs, shaping reef productivity pathways.

articles.title: Bridging the divide between models of animal‐driven and microbial‐driven biogeochemical cycling: A zoogeochemistry‐informed framework
articles.digest: A zoogeochemistry-informed framework proposes bridging animal-driven and microbial-driven models of terrestrial biogeochemical cycling, currently dominated by plant- and microbe-centric approaches.

articles.digest: Elevated UV-B altered anti-herbivore defence allocation in cereal crops, but silicon supplementation maintained resistance to insect herbivory despite UV stress.

articles.title: Intraspecific trait variation responds consistently to global change drivers and mediates herbivory across unrelated plant species
articles.digest: Intraspecific trait variation responded consistently to global change drivers across unrelated plant species, with high-elevation plants showing greater herbivore susceptibility via leaf traits.

articles.title: Historical vertebrate herbivory drives ontogenetic shifts in plant defense across oceanic islands
articles.digest: Islands with historically intense vertebrate herbivory maintained strong juvenile plant defences that declined with age, unlike islands lacking browsing history.

articles.title: Partial mycoheterotrophy: a common strategy in arbuscular mycorrhizal plants?
articles.digest: Partial mycoheterotrophy, where photosynthetic plants also acquire carbon from fungal networks, may be a common but overlooked strategy among arbuscular mycorrhizal plants.

articles.title: Ecohydrological characteristics of biocrusts explain divergent responses of biocrusts and grasses to rainfall reduction
articles.digest: A four-year rainfall-reduction experiment showed biocrust cover was more resilient than grass cover to drought, mediating water availability in a semi-arid grassland.

articles.title: Phenotypic plasticity of invasive knotweed across Europe: A distributed common garden experiment
articles.digest: A distributed common-garden experiment across Europe found invasive knotweed relies on strong phenotypic plasticity rather than local adaptation to succeed across climates.

articles.title: Global Quantification of the Ecological Footprint of Freshwater Fish Invasions
articles.digest: A global quantification found invasive freshwater fish substantially reshape biodiversity and ecosystem function, testing classic invasion theory across river systems worldwide.

articles.title: Ant‐Mediated Ecosystem Functions Along a Tropical Elevational Gradient
articles.digest: Ant-mediated seed removal and insect predation varied along a tropical elevational gradient, linked to shifts in ant phylogenetic diversity, climate and productivity.

articles.title: Unequal distribution and accessibility of landscape features in Australia’s protected areas
articles.digest: Landscape features in Australia's protected areas are unevenly distributed and accessible, with remote or restricted access limiting many geological and water features.

articles.title: Changing patterns of colonisation and persistence during the wolf recolonisation of the human‐dominated Italian alpine region
articles.digest: Six years of monitoring showed wolves recolonising the Italian Alps continue favouring natural habitat despite growing human density, with livestock proximity reducing persistence.

articles.title: Above‐ground and below‐ground productivity shows divergent compensatory responses to grazing in an alpine grassland
articles.digest: A three-year yak grazing experiment found above-ground grassland productivity strongly overcompensated for herbivory while below-ground productivity showed little compensatory response.

Are forestry clearcuts ecological traps for caribou?
articles.digest: GPS-collared caribou selected regenerating forestry clearcuts as strongly as natural habitat despite elevated wolf, coyote and bear activity, suggesting an ecological trap.

articles.title: Landscape configuration and soil properties shape ant‐mediated seed dispersal in tropical dry forest agricultural landscapes
articles.digest: Ant-mediated seed dispersal in Caatinga dry forest responded to local soil and landscape structure, with low-intensity cropland sustaining dispersal as well as natural remnants.

articles.title: Landscape optimization trade-offs and security boundary identification under multiple scenarios: balancing food production and ecological protection for regional sustainability
articles.digest: A landscape optimization approach identified spatial zones in China's Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei region where ecological indicators improve without compromising food production.

articles.title: Insights from three decades of IUCN Red List assessments catalyzing shark, ray, and chimaera conservation
articles.digest: Three decades of coordinated IUCN Red List assessments produced an unprecedented extinction-risk trajectory for sharks, rays and chimaeras, with lessons for future assessments.

articles.title: Evaluating a structured expert elicitation approach for adaptive conservation management based on lessons from five years in practice
articles.digest: Five years of New South Wales' Saving our Species program showed structured expert elicitation predictions for threatened species management can be usefully evaluated against outcomes.

articles.title: Automated mapping of dry stone walls in terraced landscapes using airborne laser scanning data
articles.digest: Airborne laser scanning combined with deep learning mapped dry stone walls across an Austrian UNESCO World Heritage terraced landscape with high accuracy.

articles.digest: A new framework combining eDNA inventories, graph theory and niche modelling was proposed to strengthen ecosystem assessment and monitoring.

articles.title: Not single large, not several small: the Requirement Estimation Based on Species Ecological Needs (REBSEN) is the best conceptual model to define conservation strategies in severely fragmented landscapes
articles.digest: A new REBSEN model was proposed to replace the decades-old SLOSS debate, arguing habitat quality and dispersal capacity better explain amphibian responses in fragmented Atlantic Forest.

articles.title: Ecosystem Synchrony Among Small Shallow Lakes is Driven Differently by Their Biotic and Abiotic Similarities

Asymmetric transferability of trait–environment relationships between native and exotic plant species in Singaporean forests differing in land‐use history
articles.digest: Trait-environment relationships in Singaporean forests did not transfer consistently between native and exotic plant species, challenging generalised trait-based distribution models.

articles.title: Extending the leaf economics spectrum: Freshwater plant communities exhibit realm‐specific leaf adaptations
articles.digest: Nearly 7,000 plant communities across European lakes and rivers showed the leaf economics spectrum needs realm-specific adaptation for aquatic plants.

articles.title: Hydraulic hubs link root-leaf traits dimensions and drive the intraspecific whole-plant traits space in alpine shrub Dasiphora fruticosa
articles.digest: Hydraulic hubs linking root and leaf trait dimensions were found to drive whole-plant trait variation within the alpine shrub Dasiphora fruticosa.

The timescale of nematode evolution reveals late and convergent radiations of parasitism
articles.digest: A time-calibrated phylogeny combining genomic data from 156 nematode species revealed a surprisingly late and convergent evolutionary origin of parasitism.

articles.title: Genomic Structure and Demographic History of the Brown Fur Seal: From the Ice Age to Current Isolation
articles.digest: Genome-wide markers revealed clear differentiation between Australian and Cape brown fur seal subspecies alongside substantial gene flow within each subspecies' colonies.

articles.title: Genomic and evolutionary basis of parthenogenesis in a disease-vector tick species
articles.digest: Comparative genomics and transcriptomics in disease-vector ticks revealed the evolutionary mechanisms separating bisexual and parthenogenetic strains of the species.

methods_radar.title: A New Framework to Empower Ecosystem Assessment Through the Integration of eDNA Inventories, Graph Theory and Niche Modelling
methods_radar.note: Introduces a new framework combining eDNA inventories, graphlet-based network analysis and niche modelling for ecosystem assessment.

methods_radar.title: Not single large, not several small: the Requirement Estimation Based on Species Ecological Needs (REBSEN) is the best conceptual model to define conservation strategies in severely fragmented landscapes
methods_radar.note: Proposes REBSEN, a new conceptual model replacing the SLOSS framework for prioritising fragmented-landscape conservation strategies.

methods_radar.title: Modelling density surfaces from integrated passive acoustic and point count data streams
methods_radar.note: Introduces a novel model integrating passive acoustic and point count data to estimate absolute animal density surfaces.

methods_radar.title: ForestForTrees: An R package to infer forest structure from remote sensing data
methods_radar.note: Introduces ForestForTrees, an R package making Bayesian forest structure inference from remote sensing accessible without specialist expertise.

methods_radar.title: Comparative species distribution model framework for marine conservation and its application to loggerhead turtles in the Mediterranean
methods_radar.note: Develops a benchmarking workflow comparing species distribution model performance for marine conservation, tested on loggerhead turtles.

methods_radar.title: Ground‐to‐canopy monitoring reveals hidden ecological patterns in Congo Basin mammals
methods_radar.note: Extends camera-trap biodiversity monitoring vertically across forest strata rather than focusing on a single arboreal layer.

methods_radar.title: Testing the Influence of Internal Stem Decay and Topography on Biomass and Carbon Stock Estimates in Mongolia’s Boreal Forest
methods_radar.note: Develops an improved stand biomass estimation approach incorporating stem decay and topography alongside standard allometric predictors.

methods_radar.title: Defaunation Maps for Tropical Mammals and Birds
methods_radar.note: Extends novel defaunation-mapping frameworks to produce pantropical maps of hunting-driven mammal and bird population declines.

methods_radar.title: Enhancing rule-based potential connectivity models for African savanna elephants (Loxodonta africana) using remote sensing in Binga District, Zimbabwe
methods_radar.note: Enhances rule-based elephant connectivity resistance models using higher-resolution remote-sensing land-cover classifications instead of coarse global maps.

methods_radar.title: Automated mapping of dry stone walls in terraced landscapes using airborne laser scanning data
methods_radar.note: Benchmarks Random Forest against deep-learning segmentation architectures for automated detection of dry stone walls from laser-scanning data.

methods_radar.title: Maximizing eDNA detections of rare marine organisms: A case study of a delphinid species
methods_radar.note: Rigorously evaluates and optimises eDNA sampling and processing methods for detecting a rare marine delphinid species.

methods_radar.title: Discovering the unseen: A performance comparison of taxonomic classification methods for unknown DNA barcodes
methods_radar.note: Benchmarks the performance of taxonomic classification methods for identifying unknown DNA barcodes lacking reference matches.

methods_radar.title: BIMr2: Inference of migration rates and their drivers from multilocus genotypes
methods_radar.note: Presents BIMr2, an improved Bayesian method for inferring migration rates and environmental drivers from multilocus genotype data.

methods_radar.title: Addressing false negatives and positives in eDNA studies
methods_radar.note: Reviews and provides guidance on addressing false positive and false negative detection errors across eDNA studies.

methods_radar.title: Resolving Emergent Patterns in Community Genetics With Environmental DNA
methods_radar.note: Extends environmental DNA methods to simultaneously characterise community composition and genetic variation, a new application of eDNA.

methods_radar.title: Physiological fidelity of a satellite-derived forest resilience indicator in the Amazon
methods_radar.note: Validates satellite-derived temporal autocorrelation against physiological hydraulic safety margins as a forest resilience indicator in the Amazon.

methods_radar.title: Pan‐boreal mapping of forest age and implications for conservation
methods_radar.note: Applies a random forest model integrating multisource datasets to map forest age across the boreal biome.

methods_radar.title: Changing patterns of colonisation and persistence during the wolf recolonisation of the human‐dominated Italian alpine region
methods_radar.note: Applies dynamic occupancy models to a multi-year dataset tracking wolf colonisation and persistence in the Italian Alps.

methods_radar.title: Species traits mediate the abundant‐center patterns in ground‐dwelling mammal and bird species in China
methods_radar.note: Applies standardised camera-trap survey data to test the abundant-centre hypothesis across ground-dwelling mammal and bird species.

methods_radar.title: Is There Mush-room for Bettongs on Dirk Hartog Island? Environmental DNA Assessment of Food Resource Availability for Fauna Translocations
methods_radar.note: Applies environmental DNA metabarcoding to assess fungal food resources ahead of a marsupial reintroduction.

methods_radar.title: Topography is a Major Determinant of Forest–Savanna Distributions in Mosaic Landscapes in Central Africa
methods_radar.note: Applies machine learning to topographic variables to predict forest-savanna vegetation states in Central African mosaics.

methods_radar.title: Estimation of Forest Soil Organic Carbon Density in Dry Tropical Valleys Based on Multi-source Data and Machine Learning Algorithm
methods_radar.note: Applies multi-source data and machine learning to estimate forest soil organic carbon density in dry tropical valleys.

methods_radar.title: From Deficiency to Enrichment: Diverging Soil Phosphorus Trajectories Across China's Croplands (1980–2018)
methods_radar.note: Applies a machine-learning framework to harmonised soil surveys to track four decades of cropland phosphorus change.

methods_radar.title: Predicting Coral Offspring Survival From Reef Thermal Histories in Ningaloo World Heritage Reef
methods_radar.note: Applies boosted regression tree analysis to satellite thermal histories to predict coral larval survival.

methods_radar.title: Strategic dam management can offset climate‐driven declines in fish production
methods_radar.note: Applies a hierarchical Bayesian stock-recruitment model to link six decades of climate and dam management to trout productivity.

methods_radar.title: Temporal and spatial trends in study designs used to understand what works in conservation
methods_radar.note: Applies Bayesian multinomial logistic regression to classify and analyse trends in conservation evaluation study designs.

methods_radar.title: Effects of urban vegetation on the surface urban heat island: a UAV approach with a focus on private gardens
methods_radar.note: Applies UAV imagery and Random Forest classification to quantify urban heat island mitigation by green infrastructure.

methods_radar.title: Spatiotemporal dynamics and cross-boundary connectivity of an ecological network across a fragmented landscape
methods_radar.note: Applies Landsat classification and Morphological Spatial Pattern Analysis to quantify four decades of forest fragmentation.

methods_radar.title: Multiscale species distribution models reveal seascape niche partitioning and overlap in native and alien fishes
methods_radar.note: Applies multiscale species distribution models to quantify niche overlap between invasive and native reef fish.

methods_radar.title: A multi-satellite comparison of forest growth between karst and adjacent non-karst nature reserves
methods_radar.note: Applies multi-satellite vegetation indices to compare forest growth between karst and non-karst nature reserves.

methods_radar.title: Farmland bird diversity requires heterogeneity between and within habitats
methods_radar.note: Applies passive acoustic monitoring and BirdNET classification to survey farmland bird diversity across habitat mosaics.

methods_radar.title: Are forestry clearcuts ecological traps for caribou?
methods_radar.note: Applies GPS telemetry and step-selection functions to test whether forestry clearcuts function as caribou ecological traps.

methods_radar.title: Quaternary Ice Ages Shaped Protists Phylogeography: The Case of Arcellinida in the Iberian Peninsula
methods_radar.note: Applies a large metabarcoding dataset to test the genetic-legacy-of-Quaternary paradigm in protist phylogeography.

methods_radar.title: Asymmetric transferability of trait–environment relationships between native and exotic plant species in Singaporean forests differing in land‐use history
methods_radar.note: Applies joint species distribution modelling to test transferability of trait-environment relationships between native and exotic plants.
